title: Karantina massacre
strengths: Provides a bounded date, location, and repeated direct-death range for the massacre.
limitations: Reference synthesis rather than a primary archival count; denominator evidence is absent.
notes: Used only as a bounded massacre range source.

title: Tel al-Zaatar massacre
strengths: Provides the widely repeated 2,000-3,000 total killed range and reports roughly 1,500 fighters inside the camp.
limitations: Aggregates siege and massacre deaths; status separation is unresolved.

title: Tall al-Zatar
strengths: Provides concise context on camp population and the final massacre-day death figure.
limitations: Short encyclopedia summary rather than a denominator study.
notes: Used for camp-population context and the about-1,500 killed-on-12-August figure.

title: Tal al-Za‘atar refugee camp
strengths: Provides contextual camp-population estimates and siege chronology.
limitations: Not a fatality registry and not a denominator reconstruction for the final massacre window.
notes: Used only as context for population-in-place uncertainty.

title: Sabra and Chatila
author_or_org: Mass Violence and Resistance Research Network / Sciences Po
strengths: Strong qualitative support that the victims were overwhelmingly civilians and that the massacre was bounded in time and space.
limitations: Not itself a denominator study and does not resolve the numerical dispute.
notes: Used mainly for status coding and episode framing.

title: Sabra and Shatila massacre
strengths: Provides the widely repeated 1,300-3,500 death range and episode dates.
notes: Used for the numerical range only, not to narrow uncertainty.

citation_short: Shahid, The Sabra and Shatila Massacres: Eye-Witness Reports
title: The Sabra and Shatila Massacres: Eye-Witness Reports
author_or_org: Leila Shahid / Journal of Palestine Studies / PalQuest mirror
strengths: Rich qualitative evidence on massacre process and overwhelmingly civilian victim profile.
limitations: Not a formal numerical enumeration and not a denominator source.
